title: Seamless state sync: ActiveRecord model sync with vindi-rails-integrations
excerpt: Learn how to synchronize ActiveRecord models with Vindi transparently, handling soft deletes, bulk updates, and reconciliation audits.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Guaranteed event delivery: Transactional outbox and resilient webhooks in vindi-rails
excerpt: Avoid billing inconsistencies caused by network timeouts. Learn how to implement the Transactional Outbox Pattern and secure webhooks in Ruby on Rails.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Decoupled and resilient recurring payments: Introducing the vindi-rails SDK
excerpt: Integrate your Rails app with Vindi's recurring billing platform. Support dynamic thread-safe multi-tenancy, built-in caching, and intelligent retries.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Distributed scheduling and crons: Solving the double-execution problem
excerpt: How do you guarantee a Cron task runs exactly once per minute across a cluster of servers without a coordinator like ZooKeeper? Learn the mechanics behind Orkai Runiq.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Resilient concurrency: FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED in Postgres and SQLite WAL in Runiq
excerpt: How do you ensure multiple distributed workers do not consume the same task without causing locking bottlenecks? Explore SKIP LOCKED and the secret of concurrent SQLite in Go.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Runiq genesis: Designing a standalone background job engine in Go
excerpt: Why build a task processor from scratch when we have RabbitMQ or Kafka? Explore the design decisions, zero-coupling architecture, and core abstractions of Orkai Runiq.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Indirect responses, query snapshots, and semantic routing in HTTP QUERY
excerpt: Explore the most advanced sections of RFC 10008. Learn how to use indirect responses with status 303, the crucial difference between Content-Location and Location, and how to apply these patterns in microservices architectures.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Advanced caching mechanics, body normalization, and security in HTTP QUERY
excerpt: Deep dive into Sections 2.7 and 4 of RFC 10008. Understand the challenges of generating stable cache keys for request bodies, payload normalization rules (+json), and crucial security guidelines.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Practical implementation: Building an HTTP QUERY server from scratch in Go and Ruby
excerpt: Put RFC 10008 into practice. Learn how to implement HTTP QUERY support from scratch in Go (net/http) and Ruby (Rack/Sinatra), handling routing, status 422, and conditional caching.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Content negotiation, conditional requests, and semantic error handling in HTTP QUERY
excerpt: Deep dive into RFC 10008. Learn how to design resilient servers by handling query syntax errors with status 415/422, managing advanced body-based caching with ETags, and serving conditional responses.
author: Wesley Lima

title: The impact of HTTP QUERY on REST API design and the redefinition of GraphQL
excerpt: How does the QUERY method transform the API ecosystem? We analyze its impact on REST search routes and how it can redefine GraphQL query delivery with native edge caching support.
author: Wesley Lima

title: The new HTTP QUERY method: Overcoming GET and POST limitations in modern APIs
excerpt: Discover RFC 10008, defining the HTTP QUERY method. Learn how it solves the dilemma of sending complex queries safely, idempotently, and with native caching support.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Disaster recovery: Reprocessing failed messages with the DLQ Redriver utility
excerpt: Failed queues (DLQs) isolate bad messages, but how do we reprocess them? Learn how to design and operate an automated Redriver in the SharedBroker gem.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Data security: Topic-specific key rotation and HTTP schema registry
excerpt: Manage contracts of distributed events and isolate cryptographic key access granularly. Learn about secret rotation and JSON Schema validation.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Optimization and idempotency: Automatic payload compression and deduplication middleware
excerpt: Large payloads and duplicate messages are common challenges in distributed systems. Learn how to compress payloads dynamically and guarantee idempotency in SharedBroker.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Concurrency control, adaptive backpressure, and hybrid multi-adapter routing
excerpt: How do you avoid overloading local databases and route events to multiple brokers in Ruby microservices? Learn about concurrency and routing.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Zero-allocation dispatcher: Routing millions of events without heap allocations in Go
excerpt: How do you design a high-performance in-memory event dispatcher? Learn how onkai-unified-bus uses sync.Pool and goroutine worker pools to bypass garbage collector cycles.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Flow control and backpressure: Preventing memory saturation in event intake loops
excerpt: What do you do when consumers cannot catch up with producers? Learn how to design resilient backpressure strategies using non-blocking buffers in Go.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Secure boundaries: Payload validation (dry-schema and HTTP registry), AES-256-GCM encryption, and key rotation
excerpt: How do you guarantee contract integrity and security for sensitive data? Learn about payload validation and encryption with rotating keys.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Resilience in Ruby microservices: Exponential backoff, DLQ, and thread-safe circuit breaker
excerpt: Distributed systems fail. Learn how to design retry policies, Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) routing, and outbound circuit breakers in SharedBroker.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Decoupling messaging in Ruby: Implementing the adapter pattern with InMemory, RabbitMQ, Kafka, and Redis
excerpt: How do you decouple message publishing and subscription from the physical broker in Ruby? Learn how to design and use multiple adapters.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Automated diagnostics: On-demand profiling with auto-triggered pprof
excerpt: Investigating CPU and memory spikes after a server crash is ineffective. Learn how to automate profiling on-demand using pprof in Go.
author: Wesley Lima

title: From custom engine to industry standard: Latency percentiles and OpenTelemetry's semantic bridge
excerpt: Why do average latency metrics lie? Discover the math behind tail-latency percentiles and how we created a semantic bridge to support OpenTelemetry with concurrent dual routing.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Resilience at scale: Building a dependency-free circuit breaker and exponential backoff retries
excerpt: Distributed systems fail. Calling unstable APIs blindly causes cascading outages. We show how to design a pure state machine circuit breaker and exponential backoff retry runner in Go.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Distributed context propagation: W3C, B3, and Go HTTP client decorators
excerpt: How do you trace transactions across network boundaries? Learn how to propagate correlation identifiers using W3C and B3 headers, decorating Go's net/http client transparently.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Say goodbye to I/O bottlenecks: Building a high-performance concurrent log ring-buffer
excerpt: Writing logs synchronously throttle your APIs. Discover how to build a non-blocking asynchronous log buffer with safe fallback under high saturation.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Building a reflection-free structured JSON logger and a LIFO tracer from scratch in Go
excerpt: How do you correlate structured logs with deep execution trees without polluting your business logic? Learn how to design a reflection-free logger and a concurrent tracer.
author: Wesley Lima

title: OTEL Protocol integration: Building a native OTLP HTTP/JSON exporter without external SDKs
excerpt: Leverage OpenTelemetry standards without bringing in heavy SDK packages. Learn how to export spans and logs natively in Go.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Local vs. production viewing: Colored ANSI formatters and self-monitoring metrics
excerpt: Structured JSON logs are perfect for indexers but hard to read in local terminals. Learn how to implement color console logs in Go.
author: Wesley Lima

title: HTTP payload logging: Implementing smart sampling to prevent storage saturation
excerpt: Logging entire HTTP request and response payloads consumes massive storage and hurts performance. Learn how to apply payload sampling in Go.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Data safety: Implementing regex-based PII masking and sanitization
excerpt: Leaking PII into log files violates regulations like GDPR and LGPD. Learn how to mask PII dynamically using Regex in Go.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Real-time alerts: Detecting slow queries and protecting databases from degradation
excerpt: Slow database queries cause silent bottlenecks and exhaust connection pools. Learn how to design an automated slow query alert system.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Baggage propagation: Sharing business metadata across microservices via W3C standard
excerpt: Telemetry traces only link function calls. Learn how to use W3C Baggage to propagate business metadata across your entire microservices mesh.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Database visibility: Building a TraceSQL context wrapper to measure query latencies
excerpt: Database queries are often the primary source of API latency. Learn how to automate SQL query tracing natively in Go.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Runtime telemetry: Building a system resource collector for the Go runtime
excerpt: How do you monitor actual memory consumption, Garbage Collector pauses, and active goroutines in Go? Create a background resource collector.
author: Wesley Lima

title: HTTP resilience: Designing a panic recovery middleware to capture crashes
excerpt: Unexpected runtime failures shouldn't take down your web server. Learn how to intercept panics and return structured JSON errors in Go.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Efficient disk writing: Building a log file rotation writer without dependencies
excerpt: Writing logs to files indefinitely eventually saturates server disk storage. Learn how to design a thread-safe, concurrent log rotation writer in Go.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Distributed transactions with Sagas: Orchestrating steps and compensations
excerpt: Distributed microservices lack unified database transactions. Learn how to use the Saga pattern to coordinate complex operations and compensation steps.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Reflection-free dispatcher: Optimizing performance and supporting native AOT
excerpt: Dynamic reflection degrades Garbage Collector performance and breaks ahead-of-time compilation. Learn how we designed a reflection-free router in Go.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Avoiding duplicate processing: The Inbox Pattern and idempotent consumers
excerpt: Duplicate messages are inevitable in distributed systems. Learn how to implement the Inbox Pattern to guarantee idempotent event consumption.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Scheduled and delayed messages: Routing with TTL and dead-letter exchange in RabbitMQ
excerpt: Need to delay message delivery by a few minutes without using additional plugins in RabbitMQ? Learn how to use TTL and Dead-Letter Exchanges.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Reliable messaging: Guaranteeing at-least-once with Transactional Outbox pattern
excerpt: How do you guarantee that an event is sent to the message broker only if the database transaction succeeds? Learn about the Transactional Outbox pattern.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Background message consumption: Designing a resilient hosted service
excerpt: How do you run continuous and asynchronous message consumption without blocking the application's main thread? Design a resilient background worker.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Asynchronous tracing: Propagating telemetry context across event boundaries
excerpt: How do you track distributed execution flows through asynchronous queues without losing correlation? Learn how to inject and extract trace contexts in message headers.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Error state machines: Designing dead-letter queues (DLQ) and jittered retries
excerpt: Distributed systems fail in unpredictable ways. Learn how onkai-unified-bus implements safe exponential backoff retries and poison pill isolation.
author: Wesley Lima

title: Decoupled drivers: Abstracting NATS, RabbitMQ, and Kafka with a unified interface
excerpt: How can you switch between different messaging providers without changing a single line of business logic? Learn about onkai-unified-bus's decoupled driver design.
author: Wesley Lima
